summary: Iteration 83: New Dedicated Expenses Page Feature Testing. All 13 features verified. Fixed error handling bug where backend error objects were rendered as React children causing app crash. New /expenses page is fully functional with nav item, expense list, CRUD operations, Farm Expense and Customer Cash Out dialogs, filters, and data consistency with old Accounting page.

test_results.Test 1 - Expenses nav item in sidebar: PASS - Found with data-testid='nav-expenses' under TRANSACTIONS section
test_results.Test 2 - Navigation to /expenses: PASS - Clicking nav item navigates to /expenses
test_results.Test 3 - ExpensesPage loads: PASS - Component has data-testid='expenses-page', loads expense data
test_results.Test 5 - Record Expense button: PASS - Opens dialog with category, description, date, amount, payment method, reference fields
test_results.Test 7 - Farm Expense button: PASS - Opens dialog with service description, notes, amount, customer select, due date, terms
test_results.Test 8 - Customer Cash Out button: PASS - Opens dialog with customer select, amount, description, notes, payment method, due date
test_results.Test 9 - Filters panel: PASS - Toggle button works, shows category, payment method, date range, search filters
test_results.Test 10 - Edit button: PASS - Opens 'Edit Expense' dialog with existing data
test_results.Test 11 - Delete button: PASS - 11 delete buttons found, triggers confirm dialog
test_results.Test 12 - Accounting page works: PASS - /accounting still shows Expenses, Receivables, Payables tabs
test_results.Test 13 - Data consistency: PASS - Expenses created on new page appear on Accounting page

bug_fix_applied.issue: Runtime error: Objects are not valid as React child - error response object rendered directly
bug_fix_applied.root_cause: Backend returns error as object with keys {type, message, cashier_balance, required, shortfall, suggestion} when cashier has insufficient funds. toast.error() was receiving this object instead of string.
bug_fix_applied.fix: Updated catch blocks to extract message from error object: const detail = e.response?.data?.detail; const msg = typeof detail === 'string' ? detail : detail?.message || 'default error';

critical_code_review_comments: ExpensesPage.js: Well-structured with proper data-testid attributes for all interactive elements
Layout.js Line 35: Correctly adds /expenses route under TRANSACTIONS section with Receipt icon
App.js Line 54-55: Correctly imports ExpensesPage and registers route at /expenses
Error handling in ExpensesPage now properly handles both string and object error responses from backend
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 83 verified the new dedicated /expenses page feature. All UI elements work correctly. A bug was found and fixed where backend error objects caused React runtime crash - now properly handled. The new Expenses page uses the same /api/expenses endpoints as AccountingPage. Both pages show consistent data. Test expenses created: TEST_EXPENSE_PAGE_83_SMALL, TEST_EXPENSE_PAGE_ITERATION_83.
